title: Neha, a prominent face of the CJP protests, presses on with hunger strike
content: The All India Students’ Association’s national president and two of her colleagues continue the hunger strike demanding reforms in education. She vows to continue the fight to remind people that ‘the divisive politics that the BJP has taught’ is not their legacy

title: Crowd swells at CJP protest site ahead of Parliament march
content: Crowds continued to pour into the CJP's protest site at Jantar Mantar through the day as preparations gathered pace for Monday's march to Parliament

title: CJP protest in Delhi LIVE: Will end fasting today if government takes accountability for failures in education system, says Sonam Wangchuk
content: The CJP’s 1.5-kilometre march is planned to start from the Jantar Mantar protest site, before moving through the Patel Chowk roundabout and terminating at the new Parliament building

title: Govt. lists five Bills for Monsoon Session, no mention of delimitation legislation
content: Government to table Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill that will make the intentional insult of, or disruption during, the singing of Vande Mataram a punishable offence

title: Jantar Mantar protest highlights: Wangchuk will end fast if Parliament session focuses on education accountability, says Gitanjali Angmo
content: The Delhi Police say ‘no permission sought or granted’ for July 20 Parliament protest march and Section 163 is currently in effect in the New Delhi district

title: The E20 transition: fuel for debate
content: As car and bike owners report clogged injectors, damaged fuel pumps, and contaminated petrol across the country, a look at competing claims on what is driving the complaints, and whether ethanol is really to blame

title: CPI(M) opposes introduction of Bill to give Vande Mataram legal protection, calls it unconstitutional
content: John Brittas filed a notice under Rule 67 opposing the introduction of the Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026, arguing it breaches the constitutional settlement on the National Song’s status

title: The fine art of creating blood donors at home; and resources for those in Chennai seeking to donate blood
content: A leitmotif among blood donation organisations is the increasing difficulty in finding people with “generous veins”; and the solution could strangely be found in the living rooms of households, as illustrated by these two accounts from Chennai of parents inspiring their children to join the cause

title: Curbing talent exodus: It’s deja vu for Indian Space Research Organisation
content: In the early 1990s, the information technology boom and campus recruitments dealt a blow to ISRO which was then on an expansion mode

title: India advises citizens to postpone travel to Iran amid rising instability
content: It advised Indians already in Iran to consider temporarily exiting the country using available flight options

title: ‘There was no need for Govt. intervention’: Wangchuk’s wife Gitanjali on Delhi HC refusing interim relief on hospital transfer
content: Gitanjali Angmo said the PIL was “wrongly interpreted” and that they will file another petition on July 20

title: Thousands gather at Jantar Mantar ahead of CJP’s march to Parliament
content: The Cockroach Janta Party’s designated protest site overflowed with unprecedented crowds following activist Sonam Wangchuk’s forced hospitalisation

title: Maharashtra panel to study ST benefits after Christian conversion allegations
content: The notice stated that Christian religious leaders, missionaries, and their institutions in Navapur taluka were using inducements to compel tribals and non-tribals to convert

title: Srinagar’s Vichar Nag temple sees puja again, L-G says ‘revival serves as a historic bridge’
content: Scores of Kashmiri Pandits attended the ceremony in an area that once hosted a number of Pandit families who had to leave their homeland due to militancy in the 1990s

title: Maharashtra govt allows hotels, restaurants, bars to remain open till 4 a.m. for FIFA World Cup final
content: The Home Department issued the directive onJuly 18) following a request from the National Restaurant Association of India (NRAI), Mumbai, which sought extension of operating hours for hospitality establishments on the night of the final

title: U.K.'s Burnham drops digital ID scheme to prioritise cost of living, say allies
content: Dubbed the ‘King of the North’ for his work as the mayor of Greater Manchester in northwestern England, Burnham will replace Keir Starmer ‌as Prime Minister on July 20, when he says he will also unveil his cabinet team of top Ministers

title: Geo-tagging of State’s forest locations opens the doors for trespassers
content: Forest department flags 23 Google Maps listings inside reserve forests in Kozhikode as misleading; officials say unauthorised map pins and uploaded photographs are encouraging illegal entry into protected areas; DFO seeks removal of the listings, warning of trespassing, public safety risks and human-wildlife conflict

title: Bihar CM opens ₹130.88-cr. link to divert floodwaters, enhance farm productivity
content: The link, part of a river interlinking project, will reduce intensity of floods in northern Bihar, says Choudhary; he also inaugurates 551 schools, lists measures to improve education infrastructure

title: At least eight dead, 15 hurt in Nagaland landslide
content: Heavy incessant rainfall and a cloudburst caused widespread destruction at various locations in and around Mon town; State announces ex-gratia of ₹4 lakh for kin of deceased, ₹74,000 for each of the injured

title: Students must unite against drugs: Minister Bindu Krishna
content: She was presiding over the distribution ceremony of the ‘Focus Kollam’ Minister’s Excellence Award-2026, organised for talented students from the Kollam constituency

title: Villagers oppose new stone quarry, crusher unit near Cheyyar in Tiruvannamalai
content: The quarry might pollute groundwater and waterbodies, and affect agricultural activities, say residents

title: Panel to consider two complaints on leasing and illegal diversion of forest land in Kerala
content: The complaints on reported illegal diversion of forest land and irregular timber auction in Chalakudy forest division and the alleged continued use of reserved forest land by Plantation Corporation of Kerala will come up before the Central Empowered Committee, New Delhi, on July 20

title: PMK organises signature campaign urging State govt. to notify extent of Pallikaranai marshland
content: Speaking during the campaign, the PMK president criticised former DMK minister P.K. Sekarbabu, who was in-charge of the CMDA, for not playing the crucial role of being the watchdog in the State for real estate development

title: Move to resume Korapuzha dredging sparks call for scientific assessment
content: The environmental organisation calls for the constitution of an expert committee, including a marine ecologist and a representative of residents, to evaluate the proposal to deepen the estuary before any further decision is taken

title: West Bengal BJP government is implementing the promise of ‘Sonar Bangla’, says Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister virtually lays the foundation stone of the ₹700-crore Amul dairy project at Sankrail in Howrah

title: Will end fast if education accountability issue raised in Parliament Monsoon Session: Wife Gitanjali conveys Wangchuk’s message
content: “Sonam has requested all of us to take out tomorrow's march peacefully and ensure that the protest is not misused,” she said, conveying his message at the Jantar Mantar protest

title: INL links ‘move’ to reconstitute Waqf Board to Munambam
content: INL leader alleges the Chief Minister conspired with the Waqf Minister and the IUML to reconstitute the Waqf Board after the existing refused to compromise on the Munambam issue

title: Jammu and Kashmir rains updates: At least 11 dead, several missing in flash floods; rescue ops. under way
content: Home Minister Amit Shah assured the L-G and the CM of all possible assistance from the Centre for the safety and security of the affected people, officials said

title: DMK, TDP should uphold their legacy of ensuring fairness delimitation, says Congress
content: Congress leader says extending the 1971 Census freeze on Lok Sabha seat distribution is essential to preserve the federal balance

title: At least 11 killed, 10 missing as heavy rain triggers flash floods in J&K
content: Initial assessment suggested that more than 100 houses and around two dozen school structures were damaged in the floods

title: CIC directs IITs to form high-level committees to curb student deaths by suicides
content: It says information on panels must be proactively disclosed on the institutes’ official websites. The direction came while disposing of appeals filed by an IIT-Kanpur alumnus, who is the founder of a non-governmental organisation engaged in student rehabilitation and mental health counselling

title: Drought report to Centre in 15 days after comprehensive survey of ground realities, says DKS
content: ‘We have asked for a comprehensive field survey in all 178 taluks, of which 158 taluks have received very average rainfall, and 20 taluks have not received rainfall at all’

title: Farmers body urges the DMK to drop its demand to form Mekedatu tribunal
content: P.R. Pandian, president of the Coordination Committee of All Farmers Associations of Tamil Nadu, says it will jeopardise the farmers of the State and is against the law and farmers’ interests

title: Martyrs’ Day 2026 turns out to be show of political strength for rival Trinamool factions in West Bengal
content: Trinamool Congress MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, who, along with 19 other party Lok Sabha MPs, has merged with the little-known Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI), said the faction would observe Martyrs’ Day at Rajghat

title: All-party meet: MPs seek debate on NEET leak delimitation, temple theft, E20
content: Parties stage a brief walkout from all-party meet in protest against government’s invitation to breakaway Trinamool faction; leaders from 50 political parties who attended the meeting seek discussion on police action at Jantar Mantar and Manipur situation also

title: CBI conducts searches across four States in ₹1,109-crore bank fraud case
content: The case pertains to alleged cheating by the accused company, Gupta Power Infrastructure Ltd. According to the agency, it had taken various fund-based and non-fund based financial facilities from Canara Bank

title: Roads turn death traps for gig workers in Kerala
content: Over 30 such workers, mostly food delivery personnel, have been killed in road accidents in the State in the past one year, as per informal data collected by a union. Time constraints coupled with heavy traffic often contribute to the higher number of fatalities, those involved in the sector say

title: Police enhance night patrol, vehicle checks across Sri Sathya Sai district
content: During the inspections, the police officials question suspicious individuals moving during midnight hours without valid reasons, collect details and fingerprints of some individuals for verification

title: 20-0: How Spain utterly owned Argentina in World Cup final and rendered Messi irrelevant
content: Spain's triumph was built not on moments of brilliance alone but on collective superiority. Luis de la Fuente's side controlled possession, dictated territory and suffocated every avenue Argentina attempted to explore. World Cup finals are often remembered for drama, controversy or moments of individual inspiration. This one will be remembered for something far rarer - complete domination. Spain didn't just beat the defending champions. They made them disappear.

title: FIFA chief guides Trump away as US president refuses to leave stage - Watch
content: Spain secured their second World Cup title by defeating Argentina 1-0 in extra time. US President Donald Trump stood with the Spanish players during the trophy presentation ceremony. Boos were heard around the stadium as Trump and Infantino walked onto the field. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in the 106th minute of the match. Trump had previously intervened in a red card controversy during the tournament.

title: Intel's fired CEO says he has a hunch when the chipmaker's downfall began
content: Intel's former CEO Pat Gelsinger believes the company's decline started years before his 2021 return. He stated that major technical decisions should not be driven solely by financial analysis. Previous leadership teams lacked the necessary technical background for crucial industry transitions. The company returned significant funds to shareholders while neglecting essential investments. Gelsinger highlighted geopolitical risks impacting global semiconductor manufacturing operations.

title: US judge to Meta's laid off employees: Cannot stop the company from job cuts
content: A US judge has allowed Meta Platforms to proceed with planned layoffs of 26 employees. These employees had sued the company alleging AI-driven discrimination in job cuts. The judge declined an emergency request to halt the layoffs while claims proceed. He stated the court may reconsider if new evidence emerges about AI usage. Meta denies wrongdoing and states human decisions guided the workforce reduction.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for this week - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: Saatvik Green Energy, and Unimech Aerospace - Motilal Oswal Wealth Management Research Desk has picked these as the top stocks to buy for the week starting July 20, 2026:

title: 58 beavers turned Mount St. Helens’ wasteland into a thriving wetland
content: More than 40 years after the Mount St. Helens eruption buried a Washington river valley under volcanic sediment, 58 relocated beavers have helped restore the damaged landscape. Their dams and wetlands brought back native plants, fish and wildlife. However, conservationists fear a planned government project to raise a sediment retention structure could undo years of natural restoration.

title: Human voices near salmon streams scare off bears and eagles, disrupting forest nutrients
content: New findings from Canadian researchers reveal that human sounds near Alaska's salmon streams significantly disturb wildlife, leading to grizzly bears avoiding feeding areas. This interference disrupts the natural nutrient transfer to surrounding forests, emphasizing the detrimental effects of human activity on essential ecosystem dynamics. Scientists propose enhanced land management practices to safeguard these critical ecological processes.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: Can your builder change project plans after selling your flat? Here's what RERA says
content: RERA protects homebuyers from unauthorized builder changes to sanctioned plans.  Promoters need buyer consent for alterations beyond minor structural necessities.  Changes to common areas require consent from two-thirds of allottees.  Builders remain liable for structural defects for five years after possession.  Homebuyers can seek relief from the Authority for unauthorized changes.

title: DMK MLA arrested over alleged threat against Tamil Nadu CM Vijay
content: Vilathikulam DMK MLA GV Markandeyan was arrested by police after allegedly making threatening and controversial remarks against Tamil Nadu chief minister C. Joseph Vijay during a public meeting in Kovilpatti. His supporters protested the arrest, while police detained him, questioned him, and planned to produce him before a court, triggering political reactions.

title: Jayden Adams' death: Girlfriend dragged into controversy after tragic passing
content: South African midfielder and winger, Jayden Adams, passed away last weekend leaving millions of fans in mourning around the world. While initially there were rumors of Jayden Adams committing suicide due to the grief he caried over his grandmother’s death last month, things have now taken a serious turn.

title: Sonam Wangchuk sets 3 conditions to end fast ahead of CJP's ‘Chalo Sansad’ march
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk will end his hunger strike on July 20. He has set conditions for ending his protest against education system failures. Wangchuk demands government accountability or parliamentary assurances from leaders. He is currently hospitalized and calls it illegal detention. A march to Parliament is planned for July 20.

title: 'Your tears are our tears, Captain': Heartbroken Messi breaks down after defeat
content: Spain secured their second World Cup title after defeating Argentina in the 2026 final. Lionel Messi's quest for consecutive titles ended in disappointment following a 0-1 extra-time loss. The Argentine Football Association shared an emotional tribute to their captain after the match. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in the 106th minute of the contest. This defeat concluded a significant era for Argentinian football history.

title: Nolan's 'The Odyssey' starring scores Rs 70 crore debut in India
content: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' has achieved a record-breaking opening weekend in India. The epic fantasy adventure starring Matt Damon crossed seventy crore rupees net. This film has become Nolan's biggest opener in India and globally. 'The Odyssey' earned over two hundred sixty million dollars worldwide in its debut. The original English version has driven most of the film's business.

title: India's bowling lacks bite without Bumrah; middle-over planning all over the place
content: England dominated the third ODI against India at Lord's, amassing a record 387/3, led by a huge opening partnership between Jacob Bethell and Ben Duckett. India's bowlers, missing Jasprit Bumrah, struggled with discipline and conceded many extras. Poor middle-over planning further exposed India's bowling vulnerabilities, raising concerns for their 2027 ODI World Cup preparations.

title: Arizona mass shooting: 10 injured after gunman opens fire in Tucson
content: A mass shooting in crowded downtown Tucson wounded at least ten people early Sunday. Police officers on patrol heard gunfire and responded to the crowded entertainment district. They encountered and shot the suspected gunman, who sustained life-threatening injuries. Nine other adults were shot and taken to hospitals in critical condition.

title: Spain dethrone Argentina! Torres delivers extra-time heartbreak to Messi's 10-man side
content: Spain secured a dramatic FIFA World Cup victory over Argentina. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in extra time, ending the match 1-0. Argentina played with ten men after Enzo Fernandez received a red card. Lionel Messi's final World Cup appearance concluded without a trophy. Spain celebrated their second World Cup title after a long wait.

title: Drought vs development: Can DK Shivakumar evade the ‘SM Krishna trap’?
content: Karnataka faces a potential drought as monsoon rainfall lags significantly behind normal levels. Chief Minister DK Shivakumar is managing this agrarian crisis, echoing his mentor SM Krishna's past struggles.  Krishna's administration faced similar droughts, which opponents used to criticize his urban development focus.  Shivakumar now balances Bengaluru's infrastructure growth with rural distress, a familiar political challenge.  Modern drought management systems offer some advantage, but rainfall deficits remain a political concern.

title: Lionel Messi, Lamine Yamal share emotional hug after Spain's World Cup triumph
content: Spain secured their second FIFA World Cup title after defeating Argentina. Substitute Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in extra time. Youngster Lamine Yamal shared a memorable moment with Lionel Messi on the pitch. The Spanish team completed a dominant performance throughout the entire match. This victory marked Spain's second time lifting the prestigious World Cup trophy.

title: Oil prices jump 3% as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent crude tops $90 per barrel
content: Oil prices climbed significantly as United States and Iran conflict intensified.  Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz faced increasing pressure and disruptions.  US military actions continued for a ninth consecutive night against Iran.  Bahrain and Kuwait reported fresh missile and drone strikes across the region.  Escalating tensions kept oil prices on an upward trajectory.

title: Stable, but weak: Doctors continue electrolyte therapy for Sonam Wangchuk
content: Sonam Wangchuk continues his indefinite fast, remaining medically vulnerable and under close hospital observation. Doctors report his vital signs are stable, but blood parameters show borderline abnormalities. A joint medical team from Safdarjung Hospital and AIIMS monitors his condition closely. His wife questioned hospital management and sought a transfer, which the court declined. Prolonged fasting beyond two weeks poses unpredictable health risks and requires constant medical attention.

title: After Skyroot, more pvt spacetech startups set to reach for skies with commercial missions
content: India's private space sector is accelerating after a historic rocket launch.  Companies are now preparing for commercial missions and reusable rocket development.  Several spacetech startups are planning multiple satellite launches in coming years.  This increased launch activity will help Indian satellite firms compete globally.  The growth unlocks new opportunities for defense and private sector applications.

title: 'A disgrace': Argentina star sent off after grabbing Spain player's throat after loss
content: Spain's 2026 FIFA World Cup celebrations were marred by chaotic post-match scenes after Argentina midfielder Leandro Paredes was sent off following a mass confrontation after the final whistle. The defending champions' frustrations boiled over after their 1-0 extra-time defeat, with Paredes dismissed for his role in the altercation as pundits Gary Neville and Alan Shearer condemned Argentina's conduct.

title: Another US service member killed during Iranian drone detonation
content: A US service member died during ordnance detonation in Iraq.  Iran launched missiles and drones toward Jordan, prompting air defense activation.  US forces conducted fresh strikes targeting Iran's military capabilities.  Shipping in the Strait of Hormuz has seen a significant decline.  The conflict intensifies with reports of a strike on Iran's nuclear plant.

title: In pics: Salman Khan shows off lean look in new photoshoot
content: Salman Khan posted a cryptic message and stylish photos on social media. His lean appearance in recent pictures has raised concerns among his devoted fans. Videos from a public event showed him looking tired, prompting well wishes. He previously shared a picture enjoying monsoon at his farm house. His upcoming film 'Maatrubhumi' release date remains unannounced.

title: FIFA World Cup awards: Full winners list as Rodri, Mbappe claim top honours
content: Spain secured the 2026 FIFA World Cup title after defeating Argentina in the final. Rodri earned the Golden Ball for his midfield dominance throughout the tournament. Goalkeeper Unai Simon received the Golden Glove for his exceptional defensive performance. Teenage defender Pau Cubarsi was named FIFA Young Player of the Year. France captain Kylian Mbappe claimed the Golden Boot with ten goals.

title: Who is Ferran Torres? Spain's World Cup final hero who broke Argentina's heart
content: Spain achieved their second World Cup title after Ferran Torres scored a late goal.  The substitute's decisive strike came in extra time against defending champions Argentina.  Torres's goal broke Argentina's stubborn defense after a dominant Spanish performance.  This victory denied Lionel Messi a chance to retain the World Cup crown.  Spain also became the first nation to hold both men's and women's World Cup titles.

title: Congress demands independent probe into donation chori
content: Opposition leaders have demanded an independent probe into Ram temple donations. They also warned the government's credibility depends on swift action regarding this issue. Activists and a political party plan a march to Parliament over paper leaks. Many opposition parties support this protest and may join the Parliament gherao. These twin issues are likely to dominate the upcoming monsoon session's first week.

title: No card for Tagliafico! Yamal challenge reignites Argentina favouritism debate - Watch
content: A controversial tackle by Nicolas Tagliafico in the World Cup final sparked debate.  Many fans questioned why the Argentina defender escaped a booking for his challenge.  This incident brought back memories of previous refereeing controversies surrounding Argentina.  Spain dominated possession and controlled the tempo during the first half.  The match remained goalless at halftime with few shots recorded.

title: 'Fabrications aimed at making bad performance': NTA warns of criminal action on AI, fake OMRs
content: The National Testing Agency has warned of strict action against fake OMR sheets. Several OMR sheets submitted for NEET-UG result grievances were found to be fake or AI-generated. Candidates submitting digitally altered documents face cancellation of results and debarment. The agency is scrutinizing all complaints and will take corrective measures if discrepancies are found. Fabricated documents are being used to challenge results after the recent retest.

title: Late drama! Enzo Fernandez sent off as Argentina reduced to 10 men before extra time
content: Argentina faced extra time with ten players after Enzo Fernandez received a red card. Spain dominated possession and shots but could not score against Emiliano Martinez. The goalkeeper made a record ten saves to keep Argentina in the match. This historic goalless final entered extra time after ninety minutes. Both teams sought to win the prestigious World Cup trophy.

title: 46% of LS MPs attended over 90% of budget session: Study
content: Smaller parties demonstrated the highest average Member of Parliament attendance during the recent budget session.  RLP and RLD achieved one hundred percent attendance for all thirty-one sittings.  BJP and Congress, the largest parties, recorded lower average attendance figures.  In Rajya Sabha, regional parties also led in parliamentary attendance percentages.  This analysis highlights attendance trends among various political parties in both Houses.

title: FIFA World Cup final: Spain choke Argentina into unwanted first-half history
content: Spain controlled the World Cup final's first half, denying Argentina any scoring opportunities.  Argentina failed to register a single shot on goal before halftime, a historical anomaly.  The match remained goalless at the interval, reflecting Spain's tactical superiority.  This marked the ninth World Cup final to feature a scoreless first half.  The tournament concluded with Spain seeking their second title and Argentina defending theirs.

title: New bills, old battles, changed equations: What awaits Parliament this Monsoon Session
content: Parliament's Monsoon Session begins Monday with a packed legislative agenda. The government aims to pass the Women's Reservation Bill after its earlier defeat. Opposition parties face electoral losses and alliance collapses, altering parliamentary arithmetic. Several key bills are listed, including the FCRA and Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhishthan. The government hopes to secure passage with adjusted strategies and potential support.

title: 'Might Be Last Call To Family': Iran's Araghchi Recalls Talks With Team Trump
content: Araghchi also rejected Washington's claims that Iran could be forced into submission through military pressure. He drew comparisons with Venezuela, where the US forced regime change with the capture of President Nicolas Maduro during a military operation in January.

title: Pledging To 'Rewire Britain', 'King of North' Andy Burnham Becomes UK PM
content: Andy Burnham, 56, faces a long list of problems, from Britain's underperforming utilities to anaemic economic growth, and must first unveil his top team of ministers - already the subject of much debate in the governing Labour Party.

title: Sonam Wangchuk Lists 3 Conditions To End Hunger Strike
content: On the morning of July 18 - the 21st day of Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike - the police removed him from the site and took him to Safdarjung Hospital.

title: 'Your Father Said Sit Inside, Have Tea': Gang Robs Rs 9 Lakh From Delhi House
content: With Harishchandra, a social activist, not at home, they spoke to him over the phone, claiming they had come for a meeting.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Markets Likely To Open Lower As Oil Tops $90 Amid US-Iran War
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Oil prices jump 3% as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent crude tops $90 per barrel.

title: Parents Sell Newborn For Rs 1 Lakh In Telangana Village
content: A newborn baby was allegedly sold for Rs 1 lakh in Telangana's Mahabubnagar district, prompting police investigation.

title: Another American Soldier Killed In US-Iran War, Was Detonating Iranian Drone
content: The soldier's death on Saturday brings the confirmed number of American military fatalities since the war began on 28 February to 17.

title: Japan Gives Crypto Financial Asset Status: What It Means For Indian Investors
content: Global crypto experts believe India possesses many of the ingredients needed to become a major crypto hub.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: US Conducts Strikes On Iran For 9th Straight Night After Another Soldier Killed
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: CENTCOM said the raids were designed to "swiftly punish Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces who launched attacks against American service members in Jordan last night."

title: Parliament's Monsoon Session To Begin Today, Intense Showdowns Likely
content: Parliament monsoon session: Intense debates on national controversies are expected to dominate the 19 sittings till the monsoon session ends on August 13

title: Sleeping Couple Falls From 4-Storey Roof in Delhi, Man Dies, Wife Critical
content: Preliminary investigation revealed that the couple had dinner with the family around 9 pm and then went to sleep on the rooftop of the house.

title: Sirens, Strikes, Shattered Deal: Middle East On Edge As US, Iran Trade Blows
content: Iran's health ministry said at least 50 people had been killed and more than 500 wounded in the latest wave of US strikes.

title: Man Dies After Fan Falls On Him In Hospital, It Blames 'Medical Condition'
content: According to eyewitnesses, the ceiling fan suddenly came loose and fell directly onto the patient's bed, striking Akbar in the abdomen. He was immediately attended to by hospital staff.

title: 5.5 Magnitude Earthquake Kills At Least 5 In Peru's Andes Region
content: The US Geological Survey reported the quake struck on Saturday at 9:24 pm, with its epicentre located 2 kilometres (1.24 miles) west-southwest of the city of Sicaya in Huancayo province. It was 10 kilometres (6.21 miles) deep.

title: Israel Vows "Full Force" Response If Iran Fires Missiles
content: "If Iran fires missiles at Israel, we will attack them with full force," Israel Katz said during a visit to a rescue service facility.

title: After 2 Fatal Shootings, US Immigration Officers Wear Body Cameras On Stops
content: Homan's comments come after federal immigration agents fatally shot two men six days apart during traffic stops in Texas and Maine earlier this month. There is no body-camera footage from ICE agents involved in those incidents.

title: Delhi's New Rooftop Solar Campaign To Offer Subsidy, Help With Installation
content: The "Solarize East" campaign will initially cover South-East Delhi, with residents being guided on government subsidies, net metering and rooftop solar installation.

title: Amit Shah backs NIA-like Bengal outfit to tackle terrorism
content: the proposal was discussed at a law-and-order review meeting in Kolkata . it is the first state to have a special investigation agency modelled on NIA . only a union Territory has such an agency, which investigates terrorism-related offences .

title: 'Excited to announce our baby boy': US Vice President JD Vance, wife Usha welcome 4th child
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. title: Net services remain suspended in J&K’s Doda 3 days after man’s death in police firing
content: internet services continue to remain suspended across doda district of J&K for the third consecutive day on Sunday . locals complained that students, remote workers and job seekers are struggling with the internet shutdown, besides tourists as they can’t make online payments through apps . sources claimed the internet speed on mobile and the broadband as well has also been restricted in Kishtwar amid the heightened security .

title: Monsoon Session begins today; Oppn set to target Centre over Ram temple row, NEET, E20
content: the stage is set for a stormy monsoon session of parliament from today . opposition leaders pushed for discussions on the alleged embezzlement of donations at the Ram temple, the NEET paper leak, the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar and the E20 petrol row . the government cautioned its rivals that any disruption of the legislature is bound to cost them politically .
